meta.name: Continuous Claude v3
meta.tagline: Memory + agents + hooks + skills scaffolding over Claude Code.
meta.description: Continuous Claude v3 (CCv3) is a scaffolding layer on top of Anthropic's Claude Code CLI. It adds persistent semantic memory, 31 specialized agents, ~90 lifecycle hooks, 145+ skills, a project knowledge tree, session continuity (handoffs + ledgers), a 5-layer AST analysis pipeline (TLDR), and a Docker-sandboxed Recursive Language Models (RLM) subsystem for analyzing very large corpora. The result is an AI coding workshop that remembers across sessions, dispatches specialist sub-agents, enforces house rules automatically, and resumes work cleanly across hours, days, and weeks.

glossary.definition: One continuous Claude Code conversation, from CLI launch to exit.
A small TypeScript program that fires on a lifecycle event (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, etc.) to inject context or block actions.
A named workflow recipe under .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md, triggered by a slash command or auto-activated by keyword/intent match.
A specialized Claude instance with a focused prompt, invoked via the Task tool to keep main context clean.
PostgreSQL table of learnings (1024-dim BGE embeddings) searchable across sessions via Hybrid RRF.
A YAML document under thoughts/shared/handoffs/ that captures session outcome (SUCCEEDED, PARTIAL_PLUS, PARTIAL_MINUS, FAILED) for resumption.
A markdown state document under thoughts/ledgers/ that preserves the working narrative for the next session.
Auto-generated .claude/knowledge-tree.json mapping project structure, navigation hints, and entry points.
Per-project ROADMAP.md auto-synced by 4 hooks; tracks goals and progress.
Autonomous development mode that delegates implementation to bounded agent loops (10/30/50 iterations by task size).
Multi-step orchestrator with explicit phase gates (recon → interview → approval → execute).
5-layer AST analysis pipeline (L1 AST → L2 Call Graph → L3 CFG → L4 DFG → L5 PDG) that returns ~1,200 tokens for what would otherwise be ~23,000 raw tokens — 95% savings.
Recursive Language Models — a Docker-sandboxed agent that loads huge contexts (≥300K chars) into a Python REPL so the root model can write code to query it instead of stuffing it into the prompt.
BGE-large-en-v1.5 embedding model, 1024-dim, runs locally with no API call.
Reciprocal Rank Fusion of BM25 text search and pgvector cosine search: score = 1/(60+text_rank) + 1/(60+vector_rank).
Model Context Protocol — Anthropic's standard for letting Claude talk to external services (GitHub, Notion, browsers, databases).

categories.label: Actor
categories.description: Humans and the Claude Code CLI itself — the entry points to the system.

categories.label: Hook Layer
categories.description: Lifecycle interceptors that inject context and enforce rules. 90+ hooks across 7 events.

categories.label: Skill Routing
categories.description: The graph engine that matches prompts to skills via keywords, intent, and prerequisite chains.

categories.label: Skills Surface
categories.description: 145+ named workflows triggered by slash commands or keyword matches.

categories.label: Agents
categories.description: 31 specialized Claude sub-agents organized into 8 task categories.

categories.label: Memory
categories.description: Persistent semantic memory — PostgreSQL + pgvector + BGE embeddings.

categories.label: Knowledge Tree
categories.description: Auto-generated project structure map for navigation.

categories.label: Continuity
categories.description: Filesystem-based state transfer: ledgers, handoffs, Ralph state.

categories.label: TLDR Stack
categories.description: 5-layer AST analysis pipeline returning structured summaries instead of raw code.

categories.label: RLM
categories.description: Recursive Language Models — Docker-sandboxed agent for very large corpora.

categories.label: External
categories.description: Services outside CCv3 — Anthropic API, Docker daemon, BGE model running locally.

nodes.label: User
nodes.plain: The human at the keyboard — the only thing in this diagram that isn't automated.
mechanics: Types prompts in natural language.
Approves plans before code is written.
Confirms risky actions like deletes and pushes.
Decides when to commit, ship, or stop.
nodes.summary: The human operator driving the session.
nodes.description: Types prompts, approves plans, accepts or rejects tool calls. The only entity outside the system; everything else is automation acting on the user's behalf.

nodes.label: Claude Code CLI
nodes.plain: Anthropic's AI coding assistant — the engine that CCv3 wraps and extends.
mechanics: Runs in your terminal in any project folder.
Reads CLAUDE.md and RULES.md on startup to learn the house rules.
Dispatches every tool call, hook event, and agent spawn.
Everything else in this diagram is layered on top of CLI events.
nodes.summary: Anthropic's coding agent — the runtime that CCv3 extends.
nodes.description: Runs in any project directory. Loads CLAUDE.md, RULES.md, and the .claude/ tree. Dispatches all tool calls, hook events, and agent spawns. Every CCv3 capability is layered on top of CLI events.

nodes.label: Hook Layer
nodes.plain: Small automated rules that fire at key moments — like lane-assist on a car. Inject context or block dangerous moves.
mechanics: 90+ tiny TypeScript programs in .claude/hooks/.
Trigger on 7 lifecycle events: session start, prompt, before/after tool use, pre-compact, agent stop, session end.
Most just inject helpful context (memory matches, ROADMAP, skill hints).
5 can outright block a tool call — e.g. blocking 'pip install' on a malicious package.
nodes.description: Hooks fire on SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, SubagentStop, and SessionEnd. PreToolUse hooks can BLOCK a tool call by returning permissionDecision='deny'. All other events inject context via hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext. Hooks are TypeScript in src/, esbuilt to dist/*.mjs, and registered in settings.json.

nodes.label: Skill Routing Engine
nodes.plain: Matches what you ask for to the right pre-written playbook before Claude responds.
mechanics: Scans every prompt for keywords and intent patterns.
Looks up 91 skills and 18 agents in skill-rules.json.
Walks a dependency graph to load prerequisite skills first.
Hands Claude a list of relevant skills to consider.
nodes.summary: Graph engine matching prompts → skills via keywords + intent + prerequisites.
nodes.description: Three-layer pipeline: (1) 7 hardcoded high-confidence regex workflow triggers (e.g. /fix, /build, /commit) auto-invoke at 90%+ confidence, (2) every prompt matched against skill-rules.json (91 skills + 18 agents) for keywords and regex intent patterns, (3) skill-router.ts resolves prerequisite chains (require/suggest) and coActivate peers, then injects suggestions into Claude's context.

nodes.label: Skills Surface
nodes.plain: Named recipes for common tasks — like /commit, /fix, /recall — so Claude doesn't have to improvise.
mechanics: 145+ workflows under .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md.
Some are slash commands you type (/commit, /ralph, /maestro).
Others auto-activate when keywords or intent match.
26 are 'arscontexta' knowledge-vault skills with prerequisite chains.
nodes.description: Skills are named recipes under .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md. Some are slash commands (/commit, /recall, /rlm-analyze, /ralph, /maestro). Others auto-activate on context match. The catalog includes meta-workflows (/build, /fix, /refactor, /review, /release) and 26 arscontexta skills with prerequisite graph fields.

nodes.label: Agent Roster
nodes.plain: Specialist AI helpers Claude can delegate work to — like a coordinator dispatching the right expert.
mechanics: 31 specialists in 8 categories: research, implementation, testing, review, debug, orchestration, docs, security.
Each runs in its own clean conversation (isolated context).
Returns only a summary so the main chat stays focused.
Always inherit Opus — never the cheaper Haiku.
nodes.description: Each agent has a focused prompt and runs in its own isolated context, returning only a summary to the main Claude. Categories: Research (scout, oracle, pathfinder, session-analyst, braintrust-analyst), Implementation (kraken, spark, architect, phoenix), Testing (arbiter, atlas, validate-agent), Review (critic, judge, liaison, review-agent, plan-reviewer, react-perf, ui-compliance, surveyor), Debug (debug-agent, sleuth, profiler), Orchestration (maestro, plan-agent, onboard), Documentation (scribe, herald, memory-extractor), Specialized (aegis, agentica-agent). Rule: never use model:haiku — always omit model parameter so agents inherit Opus.

nodes.label: TLDR Stack
nodes.plain: Compresses source code into a structured summary so Claude can read many more files per session.
mechanics: 5 analysis layers, stacked: AST → call graph → control flow → data flow → program slice.
Returns ~1,200 tokens of summary vs ~23,000 raw code — about 95% smaller.
Works for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust via tree-sitter.
Fires automatically when Claude tries to read a code file (PreToolUse:Read).
nodes.summary: 5-layer AST pipeline — 95% token savings vs raw code.
nodes.description: Layers (token budget per layer): L1 AST functions/classes/signatures (~500), L2 Call Graph cross-file (+440), L3 CFG control flow + cyclomatic complexity (+110), L4 DFG variable defs/uses (+130), L5 PDG program slicing (+150). Total ~1,200 tokens vs ~23,000 raw. Languages: Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust via tree-sitter. Activated by tldr-read-enforcer (PreToolUse:Read) and smart-search-router (PreToolUse:Grep) hooks. Additional commands: tldr structure, tldr impact, tldr dead, tldr arch, tldr diagnostics, tldr change-impact.

nodes.label: RLM Subsystem
nodes.plain: Lets Claude analyze documents too big to fit in its memory — by loading them into a sandboxed Python instead.
mechanics: Loads the huge text into a Python REPL inside a fresh Docker container.
The AI writes code to filter and query it — it never reads the whole thing.
Six safety layers: frozen policy, refuse 'local' sandbox, 300K threshold, hard caps, container isolation, vanilla fallback.
Activates at ≥300K characters; below that, falls back to cached vanilla Claude.
Hard caps: max_depth=1, 20 iterations, 480s timeout, $2 budget.
nodes.description: Implements Recursive Language Models (arXiv:2512.24601). Caller invokes rlm_complete(question, context, policy). 6-layer safety stack: (L1) frozen RLMPolicy, (L2) _assert_safe refuses sandbox='local' raising ValueError, (L3) threshold gate — <300K chars routes to vanilla cached Claude, (L4) rlms caps (max_depth=1, max_iterations=20, timeout=480s, budget=$2, max_tokens=500K), (L5) ephemeral Docker container (non-root, no host mount), (L6) fallback — any exception triggers vanilla Claude with truncated context. The root model never sees the full corpus — the REPL holds it and the model writes Python code to query it.

nodes.label: Memory System
nodes.plain: A persistent notebook of past lessons that travels with you across sessions, days, and weeks.
mechanics: PostgreSQL with 4 tables: sessions, archival_memory, file_claims, handoffs.
Every entry gets a 1024-dim semantic embedding for similarity search.
Recall combines text matching (BM25) and vector search (cosine), fused with Hybrid RRF.
Auto-extracts learnings at 4 layers (L0–L3); de-dupes at 0.85 similarity.
Quality gate auto-blocks low-signal 'NOISE' entries (score <3).
nodes.description: Four tables: sessions (cross-terminal awareness), archival_memory (learnings with 1024-dim BGE embeddings + scope tag), file_claims (per-file lock with composite PK (file_path, project)), handoffs (task completions with embeddings). Search via Hybrid RRF: score = 1/(60+text_rank) + 1/(60+vector_rank). Text rank via pg_trgm/BM25. Vector rank via pgvector cosine similarity. Dedup at 0.85 similarity. Storage layers L0-L3: L0 PostToolUse auto-extract (with NOISE quality gate <3), L1 PreCompact extraction, L2 SessionEnd lazy_memory, L3 manual /remember. Learning types: WORKING_SOLUTION, ERROR_FIX, CODEBASE_PATTERN, FAILED_APPROACH, ARCHITECTURAL_DECISION, USER_PREFERENCE.

nodes.label: Knowledge Tree
nodes.plain: An auto-generated map of your project — where things live and where new things should go.
mechanics: One .claude/knowledge-tree.json per project.
Marked stale automatically when files change.
Rebuilt on demand at the next session start (no persistent daemon).
Answers navigation questions like 'where do tests go?' or 'how do I add an API endpoint?'
nodes.description: Per-project .claude/knowledge-tree.json describes folders, components, entry points, and navigation hints. Generated by knowledge_tree.py. Marked stale by tree-invalidate hook on file edits. Regenerated lazily by session-start-init-check when missing/stale. Queryable via query_tree.py for 'where to add tests', 'add_api_endpoint', etc. No persistent daemon — fully on-demand.

nodes.label: Continuity Store
nodes.plain: Files that preserve work between sessions so you never start from zero the next morning.
mechanics: Continuity ledgers — markdown narratives of the conversation.
Handoff documents — YAML with explicit outcome (success / partial / failed).
Ralph state — autonomous-mode checkpoint so long runs survive restarts.
Survives /compact via hook-managed YAML.
nodes.description: Three artifact types: (1) Continuity Ledger — markdown narrative under thoughts/ledgers/ created by pre-compact-continuity, loaded by session-start-continuity. (2) Handoff Document — YAML under thoughts/shared/handoffs/{branch}/current.yaml created by /create_handoff skill, requires explicit outcome marker (SUCCEEDED, PARTIAL_PLUS, PARTIAL_MINUS, FAILED). (3) Ralph state — .ralph/state.json with task array, retry escalation, checkpoints. Survives /compact via hook-managed YAML handoffs.

nodes.label: ROADMAP
nodes.plain: Your living goals doc — auto-updated as work happens, always visible to Claude.
mechanics: One ROADMAP.md per project.
4 hooks keep it in sync: one for prompts, three for tool actions (plan, commit, edit).
Goals visible in context on every prompt.
Cross-project guard prevents pollution between simultaneous terminals.
nodes.description: ROADMAP.md is the living goals/status document for each project. Four hooks keep it in sync: roadmap-reconcile (UserPromptSubmit — inject current ROADMAP context), roadmap-auto-update (PostToolUse — update after plan/commit actions), roadmap-plan-sync (PostToolUse — sync plan decisions), roadmap-commit-sync (PostToolUse — sync commit outcomes). Includes cross-project contamination guard that checks plan content against project-registry.json before writing.

nodes.label: Orchestrators
nodes.plain: Two modes that drive multi-step work for you — one with checkpoints, one fully autonomous.
mechanics: Maestro: phased work with explicit gates (recon → interview → approval → execute). You approve at each phase.
Ralph: fully autonomous loop bounded by iteration limits (10 / 30 / 50 by task size).
Both delegate the actual work to the agent roster.
Ralph blocks direct edits — only agents can touch code.
nodes.description: Maestro mode (/maestro): recon → interview → approval → execute, enforced by maestro-enforcer hook — skipping interview is blocked. State in .claude/maestro-state.json. Ralph mode (/ralph): autonomous PRD-driven dev where agents do the implementation. Iteration limits: 10 (small), 30 (medium), 50 (large) — enforced by ralph-watchdog. Edits blocked by ralph-delegation-enforcer; only agents can touch code. Retry escalation: Same agent → Same+context → spark → debug-agent → BLOCKED. Survives /compact via ralph-checkpoint.py.

nodes.label: Anthropic API
nodes.plain: The cloud LLM that powers all the thinking — every prompt eventually reaches it.
mechanics: Default model: Claude Opus 4.7.
Used by the main CLI, every spawned agent, and the RLM iteration loop.
Vanilla-Claude fallback uses prompt caching for cheap, fast follow-up calls.
Authenticated via the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable.
nodes.description: Cloud LLM backend. Used by the main Claude Code CLI, by spawned agents, by RLM's internal rlms agent loop, and by the vanilla-Claude RLM fallback path (with cache_control:ephemeral for prompt caching). Authentication via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var.

nodes.label: Docker Daemon
nodes.plain: Runs the database and the sandbox where risky code is executed.
mechanics: PostgreSQL container holds memory + sessions + file claims + handoffs.
RLM sandbox container is spun up fresh per analysis call.
Sandbox is non-root, has no access to your filesystem, and is destroyed after use.
Required for CCv3 to start — if Docker is off, memory and RLM won't work.
nodes.description: Hosts two containers: (1) continuous-claude-postgres — PostgreSQL with pgvector extension serving the memory/sessions/file_claims/handoffs tables, (2) continuous-claude/rlm-sandbox:3.11 — ephemeral python:3.11-slim with dill+regex, non-root user, no host mount, spun up per RLM call. Path: 'C:/Program Files/Docker/Docker/resources/bin/docker.exe'.

flows.label: Session Start
flows.description: Cold-start sequence that re-loads context, peers, knowledge tree, and recent learnings within seconds of CLI launch.
flows.outcome: Session warmed up: peer awareness, fresh knowledge tree, prior continuity, ROADMAP context, top learnings injected.

steps.detail: Run the `claude` command in the project directory.
Loads CLAUDE.md, RULES.md, and the .claude/ tree.
session-register, session-start-init-check, session-start-continuity, session-start-recovery, smarter-everyday.
Writes id/project/working_on/last_heartbeat; SELECTs peers in last 5 min for the 'Peer sessions' banner.
If .claude/knowledge-tree.json is missing or marked stale, runs knowledge_tree.py to rebuild.
Reads latest thoughts/ledgers/CONTINUITY_*.md; session-start-recovery resumes any active Ralph state from .ralph/state.json.
Pulls the highest-scoring prior learnings from archival_memory into the session banner. ROADMAP context arrives on the first UserPromptSubmit via roadmap-reconcile (see ROADMAP Auto-Sync flow).

flows.label: User Prompt → Skill Activation
flows.description: Every prompt is routed through the skill graph engine before Claude responds.
flows.outcome: Skills resolved with prerequisites and co-activation peers; suggestions injected into Claude's context.

steps.detail: Types natural-language request or a /slash-command.
Runs 3-layer pipeline: workflow triggers (regex) → keyword/intent match → graph resolution.
91 skills + 18 agents indexed by keywords and intent regex.
Walks prerequisites.require/suggest and coActivate peers. Loading mode (eager/lazy) determines whether content is inlined.
Claude sees skill suggestions + graph metadata and decides whether to invoke them.
Skill-driven actions reach the right specialist behavior on the first try.

flows.label: Memory Recall
flows.description: On every prompt, the memory-awareness hook searches archival memory and injects relevant prior learnings.
flows.outcome: Top-K relevant past learnings appear in the prompt context as a MEMORY MATCH block.

steps.detail: e.g. 'Fix the hook that stopped firing yesterday.'
Triggers BGE embedding + Hybrid RRF search.
1024-dim vector, local inference (no API call).
Combines BM25 (pg_trgm) and pgvector cosine. score = 1/(60+text_rank) + 1/(60+vector_rank). RRF scores 0.01-0.03 are good.
Claude sees prior solutions, decisions, and patterns from past sessions before answering.

flows.label: File Edit + Cross-Terminal Claims
flows.description: Every Edit/Write checks the file_claims table to prevent two concurrent sessions stomping on the same file.
flows.outcome: File claimed for this session; concurrent sessions get a warning.

steps.detail: Tool call enters PreToolUse phase.
Composite primary key on file_claims is (file_path, project).
If row exists for another session within the heartbeat window — emit warning. If not — INSERT new claim.
User decides whether to continue if another terminal is editing the same file.

flows.label: Pre-Compact Handoff
flows.description: When the context window approaches its limit, hooks persist working state before compression.
flows.outcome: YAML handoff written to disk, handoffs table row inserted with embedding, learnings extracted from thinking blocks.

steps.detail: Claude Code emits PreCompact event before compressing history.
Two hooks run: one writes the handoff, the other extracts learnings from <thinking> blocks.
thoughts/shared/handoffs/{branch}/current.yaml with goal/done/next/blockers/decisions.

steps.action: INSERT into handoffs table with embedding
steps.detail: Searchable later via the same Hybrid RRF used for archival_memory.
store_learning.py with auto-classified type and scope tag.

flows.label: Learning Storage (L0-L3)
flows.description: Four capture layers extract learnings automatically (and one manual). Quality gate filters NOISE.
flows.outcome: Deduped learning written to archival_memory with 1024-dim embedding and scope tag.

steps.detail: Tool use patterns, debugging successes, architectural decisions, user corrections.

steps.action: Trigger fires at one of four layers
steps.detail: L0 PostToolUse auto-extract, L1 PreCompact extract, L2 SessionEnd lazy_memory, L3 manual /remember.
Prevents low-signal entries from polluting recall results.
Tag determines whether the learning surfaces in this project only or across all projects.
Local inference; no Anthropic call.
If a near-duplicate exists, skip the insert.
Available to all future sessions via /recall and the memory-awareness hook.

flows.label: Knowledge Tree Regeneration
flows.description: Trees are marked stale on edits and rebuilt lazily on next session start — no persistent daemon.
flows.outcome: Fresh .claude/knowledge-tree.json with current structure and navigation hints.

steps.detail: Any Edit/Write/MultiEdit triggers PostToolUse.
Updates a marker file or stale flag in the tree JSON.
On the next session, the init-check hook runs the regen path.
Walks the project, extracts components, writes .claude/knowledge-tree.json. Validated by tree_schema.py.

flows.label: ROADMAP Auto-Sync
flows.description: Four hooks keep ROADMAP.md aligned with plans, commits, and active goals — across multiple terminals.
flows.outcome: ROADMAP.md updated on every significant action; current goals always visible in context.

steps.detail: Any of these triggers ROADMAP sync hooks.
UserPromptSubmit — Claude sees current goals on every prompt.
PostToolUse — incorporates new decisions.
Cross-project contamination guard verifies plan content matches project-registry.json before writing.
Living document tracks goals, completed items, and planned work.

flows.label: Direct Agent Delegation
flows.description: Claude routes tasks to specialist agents to keep main context clean.
flows.outcome: Agent returns a focused summary; main thread stays coordination-only.

steps.detail: Reading 3+ files, external research, complex implementation, testing, debugging, code review.

steps.action: Spawn the right specialist via Task tool
steps.detail: scout for codebase exploration, oracle for external research, kraken for implementation, etc. Always omit model parameter (never Haiku).
Each agent has its own focused prompt; isolated from main conversation.
Main context stays clean — only the synthesized answer comes back.

flows.label: Maestro Multi-Step Orchestration
flows.description: Phase-gated workflow for complex tasks: recon → interview → approval → execute. Each gate is hook-enforced.
flows.outcome: Multi-step task executed with explicit user approvals at each phase.

steps.detail: Complex multi-step task that benefits from structured orchestration.
Read context, current state, relevant code.
Ask clarifying questions. maestro-enforcer hook blocks agents until interview completes.
Present plan. Wait for user approval before execution.
Maestro dispatches work to scout/kraken/spark/arbiter/critic etc.
State tracked in .claude/maestro-state.json across phases.

flows.label: Ralph Autonomous Loop
flows.description: Fully autonomous PRD-driven development. Edits blocked at the main thread — only agents touch code. Bounded iterations with retry escalation.
flows.outcome: Feature implemented end-to-end with iteration tracking and structured handoff.

steps.action: Invoke /ralph with a PRD or task spec
steps.detail: Approve the proposed plan to enter autonomous mode.
PreToolUse:Edit|Write now blocks direct edits — main Claude must delegate.
10/30/50 iterations for small/medium/large tasks. ralph-watchdog enforces the cap.
kraken (implementation) + arbiter (tests) + critic (review). Agents can edit freely; main thread cannot.
Same agent → Same+error context → spark → debug-agent → BLOCKED (user intervention).
ralph-checkpoint.py commits; ralph-task-monitor updates state. Survives /compact via YAML handoffs.

flows.label: TLDR Read Enforcement
flows.description: Reads of analyzable source files are intercepted and replaced with 5-layer AST summaries — 95% token savings.
flows.outcome: Claude gets ~1,200 tokens of structured analysis instead of ~23,000 raw tokens.

steps.action: Claude calls Read on a .py/.ts/.go/.rs file
steps.detail: Standard tool call enters PreToolUse.
Checks whether TLDR summary is available for the file type.
L1 AST (~500 tok) + L2 Call Graph (+440) + L3 CFG (+110) + L4 DFG (+130) + L5 PDG (+150) = ~1,200 total.
Claude sees functions, calls, complexity, data flow, slicing — can drill down via tldr extract or full Read if needed.

flows.label: RLM Analysis (Huge Corpus)
flows.description: Question over a ≥300K-char corpus is routed into a Docker-sandboxed agent that holds the data in a REPL — the root model never sees the full text.
flows.outcome: RLMResult with answer + trajectory; falls back to vanilla cached Claude on any failure.

steps.detail: Pass a path/corpus and a question.
Refuses sandbox='local' (raises ValueError — 'RCE-by-LLM').
If context < 300K chars → route to vanilla cached Claude (path='vanilla-threshold').
continuous-claude/rlm-sandbox:3.11, non-root, no host mount, ephemeral. rlms agent caps: max_depth=1, max_iters=20, timeout=480s, budget=$2.
Root model sees only metadata ('context is a str with N chars'); it writes Python code, the REPL runs it, results stream back. Up to 20 iterations.
Model can spawn sub-calls on specific chunks; results injected back into REPL.
On FINAL → RLMResult{path='rlm'}. On timeout/budget/exception → truncate to 900K chars + vanilla cached Claude (path='vanilla-fallback').

flows.label: Cross-Session Recovery
flows.description: Session N+1 reconstructs full working context in seconds by loading ledger + memories + Ralph state + tree + ROADMAP.
flows.outcome: New session is fully warmed up: prior decisions, active tasks, and goals all present in context.

steps.detail: User runs `claude` after closing Session N (intentionally or via context exhaustion).
Reads latest thoughts/ledgers/CONTINUITY_*.md narrative.
Hybrid RRF over handoffs + archival_memory surfaces recent relevant decisions.
If .ralph/state.json exists, resumes the in-progress iteration loop.
Regenerates if stale; otherwise loads as-is.
Goals visible in context from the first prompt.

steps.action: Ready to pick up where Session N left off
steps.detail: Sub-minute recovery for multi-day work.

flows.label: Plan → Ralph Enforcement
flows.description: Once a plan is approved via ExitPlanMode, direct code edits on .ts/.py/.go are blocked unless Ralph is active. Forces delegation.
flows.outcome: Code edits routed through Ralph agents; config/doc edits pass through unchanged.

steps.detail: Plan written to .claude/plans/<name>.md; user approves.
$TEMP/claude-plan-approved-*.json marks the plan-approved condition.
Claude tries to apply the plan directly.
Reads plan-approved state. Checks file extension.
Code file (.ts/.py/.go) + Ralph not active → DENY with permissionDecision='deny'. Config/doc (.md/.json/.yaml) → ALLOW. Ralph active → ALLOW.
User runs /ralph to enter autonomous mode, or deletes the state file to bypass.
